perturbation.original_text: Owner warr ants that it has the right to disclose the Proprietary Inform ation to Recipient. All inform ation is pro vided "AS IS" without an y warranty regarding its a ccuracy or performance and will remain the pr operty of the O wner and/or its licensors.
perturbation.changed_text: Owner warrants that it *does not* have the right to disclose the Proprietary Inform ation to Recipient. All inform ation is pro vided "AS IS" without an y warranty regarding its a ccuracy or performance and will remain the pr operty of the O wner and/or its licensors.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Owner warrants that it has the right' to 'Owner warrants that it *does not* have the right' creates a direct contradiction. The original clause suggests the Owner is authorized to share the information, while the changed clause denies this right. This creates uncertainty about the legitimacy of the information exchange and completely negates the Owner's part of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Owner” means the party who discloses Propr ietary Inform ation. “Recipient” means the party who receiv es Proprietary Inform ation of or from the Owner. Owner warrants that it *does not* have the right to disclose the Proprietary Inform ation to Recipient. All inform ation is pro vided "AS IS" without an y warranty regarding its a ccuracy or performance and will remain the pr operty of the O wner and/or its licensors. No license or other ri ghts to Proprietary Inform ation are granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "Owner warrants that it *does not* have the right to disclose the Proprietary Information to Recipient", while the contradicted text defines 'Owner' as the party who discloses Proprietary Information. If the Owner warrants they do not have the right to disclose, it directly contradicts the definition that identifies them as the disclosing party, creating a strong in-text contradiction. Also, the Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text. Because of the change, the Owner is defined as someone who 'does not' have the right to disclose, but is also defined as the one who discloses.

perturbation.original_text: No license or other ri ghts to Proprietary Inform ation are granted or implied under this Agreement. 3. The Proprietary Information will be marked by the Owner as confidential at the tim e of disclosure. I f Proprietar y Inform ation is disclosed orally , electroni cally or wit hout an y marking, Owner will identif y it as confidential at the time of disclosure and will pr ovide a written confirmation to Recipien t within thirty days of disclosure that such inform ation is confidentia l.
perturbation.changed_text: No license or other ri ghts to Proprietary Inform ation *are* granted or implied under this Agreement. 3. The Proprietary Information will be marked by the Owner as confidential at the tim e of disclosure. I f Proprietar y Inform ation is disclosed orally , electroni cally or wit hout an y marking, Owner will identif y it as confidential at the time of disclosure and will pr ovide a written confirmation to Recipien t within thirty days of disclosure that such inform ation is confidentia l.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'No license or other rights...are granted' to 'No license or other rights *are* granted' completely reverses the intent of this section. Originally, the agreement stated that no rights were given. With the change, it implies that rights *are* granted, creating confusion about the recipient's usage rights of the proprietary information. The change makes it unclear about each partys expectation around legal protection of their IP.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All inform ation is pro vided "AS IS" without an y warranty regarding its a ccuracy or performance and will remain the pr operty of the O wner and/or its licensors. No license or other ri ghts to Proprietary Inform ation *are* granted or implied under this Agreement. 3. The Proprietary Information will be marked by the Owner as confidential at the tim e of disclosure. I f Proprietar y Inform ation is disclosed orally , electroni cally or wit hout an y marking, Owner will identif y it as confidential at the time of disclosure and will pr ovide a written confirmation to Recipien t within thirty days of disclosure that such inform ation is confidentia l.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'No license or other rights to Proprietary Information are granted' to 'No license or other rights to Proprietary Information *are* granted'. This directly contradicts the original intent, which explicitly denied any rights to the recipient. The contradicted text includes the changed text, but the surrounding clause, 'All information is provided 'AS IS' without any warranty...and will remain the property of the Owner', emphasizes that there should be no transferred right. The introduction of the phrase 'rights are granted' directly opposes this clause, creating a clear contradiction about the recipient's rights regarding the proprietary information.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; or disclosed by the R ecipient with the written approval of the Owner; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient;
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; *or disclosed by the Recipient without the written approval of the Owner*; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient;
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'disclosed by the Recipient with the written approval of the Owner;' to 'disclosed by the Recipient *without* the written approval of the Owner' creates a direct conflict. Originally, information disclosed *with* approval was exempt from the NDA. Now, information disclosed *without* approval is exempt. This severely weakens the NDA's protection, making it nearly useless because it implies that disclosure without approval is exempt, nullifying confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; *or disclosed by the Recipient without the written approval of the Owner*; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient; c. beco mes known to the Recipient from a source o ther than the Owner with out breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; d. is develope d independen tly by the Recipient withou t use of the Owner’s Propri etary Inform ation; or e. is disclosed p ursuant t o judicial or governm ental order, provided that t he Recipient notifies the Owner so that an adequate opp ortunit y is gi ven to respon d to such order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5a of the agreement to state that obligations of parties do not apply to information disclosed by the recipient *without* written approval of the owner, whereas previously it was *with* written approval. The contradicted text is in the same location and is essentially the changed text, therefore the contradiction is between what the agreement stated originally vs what it states now. This creates a direct conflict and contradicts the confidentiality of the information because disclosing without approval is now an exception to the agreement.
